channel.title: Frontion News - Global Geopolitics Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news and strategic analysis from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🇻🇪 Exxon Negotiates Return to Venezuela in Major Policy Reversal
item.description: US oil giant Exxon is in talks to resume operations in Venezuela, ending a years-long standoff and marking a significant shift in energy diplomacy under the Trump administration.

item.title: 🛢️ IEA Chief Warns Oil Markets Nearing Red Zone as Iran Crisis Continues
item.description: International Energy Agency head warns that ongoing tensions with Iran are pushing global oil markets toward critical levels, with potential supply disruptions from the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: 🦠 Uganda Restricts Travel with Congo Over Ebola Outbreak
item.description: Uganda has suspended flights to and from the Democratic Republic of Congo as an Ebola outbreak spreads to a rebel-controlled province, with 139 suspected deaths reported.

item.title: 🇨🇺 Russia and China Condemn US Over Raul Castro Indictment
item.description: Moscow and Beijing criticize Washington decision to charge former Cuban leader Raul Castro with conspiracy to kill US nationals, calling it a pretext for escalation against Cuba.

item.title: ⚖️ Top UN Court Rules Workers Right to Strike Protected
item.description: International Court of Justice issues nonbinding ruling that workers right to strike is protected under key treaty, expected to influence global labor relations.

item.title: ✈️ Chinese Firm Breaks Ground on $5B Aviation Complex in UAE Amid Iran War
item.description: A Chinese state-owned company has launched construction of a massive aviation complex in the UAE, signaling continued economic confidence in the region despite ongoing conflict risks.

item.title: 🚢 Chinese Container Ship Among Few Vessels Crossing Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A Chinese-operated container ship was one of the few vessels to cross the Strait of Hormuz in the past 24 hours, as the critical waterway remains largely blocked amid the Iran conflict.

item.title: ⚔️ US Navy Accelerates Laser Weapons Fleet After Iran Conflict Success
item.description: Operation Epic Fury has strengthened the case for laser weapons, and the US Navy is now pushing to equip every ship with directed energy capabilities.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇵🇸 U.S. Threatens to Revoke Palestinian UN Delegation Visas
item.description: The Trump administration warned it would revoke visas for the Palestinian UN delegation if the ambassador pursues candidacy for the UN General Assembly vice presidency. The threat reflects escalating U.S. pressure on Palestinian diplomatic representation.

item.title: 🇨🇺 GAESA: The Military Conglomerate at Center of U.S.-Cuba Tensions
item.description: A Reuters explainer on GAESA, the Cuban military-controlled business empire that has become a focal point in U.S.-Cuba tensions as Secretary Rubio blames the conglomerate for Cuba’s economic crisis.

item.title: ⚖️ Two Men Charged Under New Federal Deepfake Law
item.description: Federal prosecutors charged two men with using AI to create nude videos and photos under a new law targeting deepfake content. This marks one of the first prosecutions under the "Take It Down" legislation.

item.title: 🇨🇺⚠️ Rubio Doubtful on Cuba Diplomacy as Trump Raises Military Threat
item.description: Secretary Rubio expressed skepticism about diplomatic engagement with Cuba while President Trump again raised the possibility of U.S. military intervention, following criminal charges against former Cuban leader Raúl Castro.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Supreme Leader orders uranium to remain in country
item.description: Ayatollah Khamenei issued a directive that Iran's near-weapons-grade uranium must not be sent abroad, hardening Tehran's stance against a key U.S. demand in peace talks.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan President open to direct talks with Trump
item.description: Taiwan's foreign ministry said President Lai Ching-te would welcome a call with President Trump, which would be unprecedented as China claims the self-governing island.

item.title: ⚠️ Russia launches nuclear exercises with Belarus as NATO tensions rise
item.description: Russia deployed nuclear-capable missiles and issued nuclear munitions to Belarus units in major exercises involving 64,000 personnel, amid heightened tensions over Ukraine and Baltic drone activity.

item.title: ✈️ NATO jets scramble as Baltic states detect drones
item.description: Latvia and Lithuania detected drones in their airspace and urged residents to seek shelter while NATO fighter jets scrambled to intercept, the latest security incidents in the Baltics.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Bank of Japan policymaker calls for rate hike citing war-driven inflation
item.description: BOJ board member Junko Koeda said the central bank should raise interest rates as Middle East war price pressures may push inflation above the 2% target, strengthening the case for a June hike.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkish court annuls main opposition party congress in blow to CHP
item.description: An Ankara court suspended the leadership of Turkey's main opposition CHP, which defeated Erdogan's AKP in 2024 local elections. The ruling removes the party that won major victories in Istanbul and Ankara from power.

item.title: 🇲🇾 Malaysia demands TikTok explain AI deepfake account insulting king
item.description: Malaysia's government summoned TikTok over a fake account using AI to create offensive content about the king, including false claims about eating pork and images with his face on animal bodies.

item.title: 🇹🇭 Thai princess Bajrakitiyabha health declines amid multiple infections
item.description: The medical condition of Thai Princess Bajrakitiyabha has worsened due to multiple infections affecting several organs. The 44-year-old princess has been hospitalized since December 2022 after suffering cardiac arrest.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇳 US seeks major expansion of energy exports to India, says Rubio
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced the US wants to significantly expand energy exports to India amid the Iran crisis disrupting traditional supply routes. The move aims to strengthen US-India energy security ties.

item.title: 🇪🇪🇺🇦 NATO jet downs suspected stray Ukrainian drone over Estonia
item.description: A NATO fighter jet intercepted and shot down a suspected Ukrainian drone that strayed into Estonian airspace. The incident highlights growing risks as Ukraine escalates long-range drone strikes on Russian infrastructure.

item.title: 🇵🇰 Pakistan Mediates as Iran Hardens Stance on Uranium in Peace Talks
item.description: Pakistan intensified diplomacy to advance US-Iran peace negotiations, but Tehran appeared to harden its position on nuclear materials. Trump warned of military action if he does not get the right answers, while Iran reviews the latest US proposal.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇷🇺 Kremlin Dismisses Report of China Training Russian Soldiers for Ukraine
item.description: The Kremlin rejected as false a Reuters report that China's army secretly trained about 200 Russian soldiers in China late last year, some of whom went on to fight in Ukraine. The accusation adds to Western concerns about deepening China-Russia military ties.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Diplomacy Unravels Under Trump, Reshaping Global Alliances
item.description: A Reuters investigation finds that Trump's threats, personal envoys and hollowed-out embassies are reshaping US diplomacy. Allies from Europe to Asia are forging new channels as decisions increasingly driven by personalities, not institutions.
